nearest_partial_multiresolution_benchmar.reported_result: The two rendered sample-90239 transmission spectra shift relative to one another; the authors identify the shift as evidence of lateral composition nonuniformity. No noticeable change is reported for sample 90211 over the same beam-diameter change.
nearest_partial_multiresolution_benchmar.prohibited_inference: specimen point variance, correlation length, covariance family, or three-scale closure

nearest_detector_pixel_scale_benchmark.permitted_inference: source-bounded detector-pixel-scale absorption and sampling benchmark
nearest_detector_pixel_scale_benchmark.prohibited_inference: treating 400 spectra as 400 probe scales, specimen covariance recovery, or direct multiresolution validation

minimum_gopal_data_upgrade: original numerical wide- and focused-beam spectra
coordinates proving the same sampled region
measured or reconstructable beam kernels rather than nominal diameters alone
specimen thickness and depth model for sample 90239
a third calibrated scale for covariance-family closure
minimum_phillips_data_upgrade: original numerical spectra with physical coordinates
at least two additional calibrated effective scales for family closure
provenance for source-intensity drift and spatial-drift correction
claim_boundaries: A dense raster at one measurement kernel remains one effective probe scale.
Four hundred spectra do not constitute four hundred independent probe scales.
Two scales can establish qualitative scale dependence but cannot test covariance-family closure.
Rendered spectra and maps are not original numerical arrays.
A reported beam diameter is not a complete measured point-spread function or Gaussian standard deviation.
Multiple modalities do not count as multiple probe scales.
No specimen covariance parameter, covariance family, detector result, novelty claim, or manuscript authorization is established.
